Text: Frankly speaking, in terms of CPU alone, the 12900H isn't much stronger than the 12700H; it only has an increased clock speed and can be considered a pseudo-i9. However, its price is higher than that of an i7. If all other specifications were the same, buying an i9 would be like paying a tax on intelligence. The real i9 is the 55W Alder Lake-HX. With your budget, I would wait until new laptops are released in February or March, when the 13700H will be stronger than the 12900H.
